findings.evidence: grep hit: line 57 defines `theorem isSouthWest_iff ... : IsSouthWest p q ↔ p.1.val < q.1.val ∧ p.2.val < q.2.val` with no annotation.
findings.fix: Annotate the coordinate iff appropriately, at least `@[simp] theorem isSouthWest_iff ...`, and add `@[grind]` if this relation is expected to drive `grind` in rectangle/counting proofs.
findings.issue: `GridPoint.IsSouthWest` has its coordinate normal form, but `isSouthWest_iff` is not a simp/grind lemma, so automation will not reduce the base relation to the two coordinate inequalities.

findings.evidence: grep hits: lines 106 and 166 provide singleton lemmas for `I` and `JNum`; only line 172 has `J_singleton_self`, not the general `J {p} {q}` case.
findings.fix: Add a public `@[simp]` lemma such as `J_singleton_singleton` expressing `J {p} {q}` as the half of the two southwest indicators, plus any comparable-point corollary needed by grading computations.
findings.issue: `GridPoint.J` lacks the singleton characteristic lemma; the file exposes `I_singleton_singleton` and `JNum_singleton_singleton`, but users cannot simplify `GridPoint.J {p} {q}` without unfolding `J` and doing rational coercion/division bookkeeping.

findings.evidence: grep hits: lines 209, 223, and 227 define `GridState.J`, `GridDiagram.JO`, and `GridDiagram.JX`; lines 213, 231, and 235 only add commutativity variants.
findings.fix: Add named defining restatements, preferably simp-normal forms, for `GridState.J x y = GridPoint.J x.pointSet y.pointSet`, `GridDiagram.JO G x = GridPoint.J x.pointSet G.OSet`, and `GridDiagram.JX G x = GridPoint.J x.pointSet G.XSet`.
findings.issue: The state/marking specializations `GridState.J`, `GridDiagram.JO`, and `GridDiagram.JX` expose new public abbreviations but only provide commutativity lemmas, so later `M_O`/`M_X` definitions must unfold bodies to relate them to `GridPoint.J`.

summary: The new names expose the intended point-set counts, but the public API still forces downstream users to unfold bodies for the basic normal forms and specializations. Add the missing characteristic lemmas and automation annotations before building later grading definitions on these names.
